$comment: Diffract universal tool registry. Each entry defines a third-party CLI the agent can use, across four layers. INSTALL fields (repo/ref/kind/patch/build/entry/bin) are consumed at image-build time by install-diffract-tools.sh to bake the tool into /sandbox/.diffract-tools/<name> and put <bin> on the agent PATH. CONNECT fields (secretEnv/configEnv/apiHosts/binaries/authHeader) are consumed at runtime by diffract-tool-connect.sh to wire the tool's credentials securely (OpenShell generic provider -> placeholder in sandbox -> real secret injected at egress; egress allowlisted to apiHosts, attributed to binaries). ADVERTISE fields (the optional 'skill' object: name/title/summary/tags/examples) are consumed at image-build time by advertise-diffract-tools.sh to emit a hermes SKILL.md under /sandbox/.hermes/skills/diffract-tools/<name>, so the agent DISCOVERS the tool in its skill catalog and knows it is pre-authenticated (install/connect make a tool runnable; advertise makes the agent aware of it). 'patch' is an optional shell command run after clone and before build, in the tool dir — use it for proxy-compat fixups (the sandbox routes egress through an MITM proxy; node tools must use undici/fetch, so an axios tool needs adapter:'fetch'). Add a tool = add an entry here; no Dockerfile edit.

tools.name: facebook
tools.description: Facebook & Instagram — read and reply to Page/Instagram comments (incl. comments on live ads). Pre-authenticated via the operator's Facebook login; the agent uses the simple `fb` command.

skill.name: facebook
skill.title: Facebook & Instagram
skill.summary: Read and reply to Facebook Page and Instagram comments, including comments on live ads. Pre-authenticated. Use the `fb` command with plain arguments — never curl, never a token, never a URL.
examples: # Use the `fb` command. It is already authenticated — do NOT write curl, do NOT use a token,
# do NOT build a URL or use ? or &. Pass plain arguments only (fields are comma-separated;
# params are key=value). Replace UPPERCASE words with a real id from a previous result.
# Which Facebook Page and Instagram account do I manage?
fb me id,name,instagram_business_account
# My latest Facebook posts:
fb me/feed id,message,created_time limit=5
# Comments on a post (POST_ID from the result above):
fb POST_ID/comments id,message,from
# Reply to a Facebook comment (id, then the message — spaces are fine, no quotes needed):
fb reply COMMENT_ID Thanks so much for reaching out!
# Instagram media (use the instagram_business_account id from the first command):
fb IG_USER_ID/media id,caption,comments_count limit=10
# Comments on an Instagram post, then reply to one:
fb IG_MEDIA_ID/comments id,text,username
fb igreply IG_COMMENT_ID Appreciate your comment!
# Comments on LIVE ADS (ad comments live on the ad's underlying post):
fb me/adaccounts id,name
fb AD_ACCOUNT_ID/ads name,effective_status,creative{effective_object_story_id} limit=25
fb OBJECT_STORY_ID/comments id,message,from
# then reply with:  fb reply COMMENT_ID your message here
